dosers.accuracy: ±0.05mL average deviation over a 10mL dose; 0.2-1000mL per head in 0.1mL increments
dosers.reservoirNotes: No built-in reservoir — doses from external containers you supply
knownIssues: One reported calibration inconsistency traced to uneven tubing length, not the unit itself — generally the lowest complaint volume of any doser in this comparison

dosers.accuracy: ±0.05mL average deviation over a 10mL dose; same 0.2-1000mL/head range as ReefDose 2
dosers.reservoirNotes: No built-in reservoir; dosing tubing sold separately
knownIssues: Same calibration-sensitivity pattern as the ReefDose 2 — no major hardware-failure pattern found

dosers.accuracy: 0.1mL resolution; 24V stepper motor + planetary gear, adjustable flow up to ~80mL/min
dosers.app: GHL Connect app, myGHL cloud, or web interface
dosers.reservoirNotes: No built-in reservoir specified — external containers
knownIssues: WiFi reconnect complaints — some owners report needing a daily power-cycle to stay connected
No per-pump chemical labeling in the app, per owner feedback

dosers.accuracy: 0.05mL accuracy, 0.1mL resolution — Pro variant adds 2 extra pumps (7 total) for auto water-change or continuous dosing beyond the core 5
dosers.app: Focustronic All in One app (also controls their Alkatronic, Mastertronic, Powertronic, Solartronic line)
dosers.reservoirNotes: No built-in reservoir; app tracks remaining volume against a container size you enter
knownIssues: Pump heads sit close together, forcing tight tubing bends to connect hoses
When slaved to their Alkatronic auto-doser, all controlled pumps are forced to the same dose amount — can't run different Ca/Alk/Mg ratios without a manual dilution workaround
More vocal dissatisfied-owner threads than the other brands here, citing accumulated support friction

dosers.accuracy: ±0.01mL — the tightest published accuracy of any doser in this comparison
dosers.reservoirNotes: No built-in reservoir; app estimates remaining fluid from a container volume and fill level you enter
knownIssues: Manufacturer (Reef Factory, Poland) entered bankruptcy proceedings, publicly reported May 2025. The company stated its app/cloud and support would keep operating and distributor stock would sell through, with acquisition talks reported as ongoing — no confirmed resolution as of this writing. The unit is still an active, orderable SKU with no discontinuation notice, but long-term support and warranty service carry real uncertainty a buyer should weigh before choosing this over a financially stable brand.

notStandalone.note: $374.99 buys only the dosing pump — it cannot be programmed or run at all without a separate Neptune Apex A2 or A3 Series controller (own listing, $360-$1,170+) to host it. If you already run an Apex, DOS is the natural add-on; if you don't, the real price to get dosing working is DOS plus a controller you may not want yet.

An expansion module, not a doser you can buy and run on its own — it must be paired with a Doser 3 SA unit or a GHL ProfiLux controller (v4+). No USD price found at any US retailer; only listed in EUR on GHL's own store.

Confirmed discontinued — CoralVue lists remaining stock as a closeout with a Retired tag. Its successor (F4 Pro V2) exists but current US pricing, accuracy spec, and app name couldn't be confirmed at any major retailer as of this research — don't assume it's a like-for-like replacement until you've checked current numbers yourself.

kalkwasserNotes: None of the dosers above are purpose-built kalkwasser hardware — they're liquid dosers that can push a kalkwasser (calcium hydroxide) slurry through a line, and several are used that way. But kalkwasser needs two things a standard two-part comparison misses: a stirred reservoir (the slurry settles and needs continuous mixing or the dosed concentration drifts) and ideally pH-gated dosing (only dosing when tank pH is below a setpoint, to avoid the pH spikes kalkwasser is prone to). None of these dosers do pH-gating natively — DOS (via an Apex with a pH probe) and GHL's Doser (via ProfiLux's pH modules) are the two with a realistic path to it, since they already sit inside a broader sensor ecosystem. Budget for a stirring kalk reactor as a second purchase, not just a doser.
